topic: A teacher receives an unexpected gift from a former student
raw_output:
[story 1]

Mrs. Albright, usually a picture of quiet composure, nearly dropped her lukewarm coffee when the small, intricately carved wooden bird landed on her desk. It was a gift, clearly from Leo, a student she hadn't seen in five years. Her hands, usually steady when grading papers, trembled slightly as she picked it up. A wide, bright smile stretched across her face, illuminating the usually reserved lines around her eyes. "Leo? How... how did you find me?" she managed, her voice a little higher than usual. She turned the bird over in her palm, a genuine spark igniting in her chest. This was wonderful.

[story 2]

I stared at the package, my heart thumping a rapid rhythm against my ribs. It was addressed to me, handwritten in Leo's familiar, looping script. When I finally tore the paper open, the sight of the antique astronomy book inside made me gasp. It was a treasure. I immediately began flipping through the pages, my fingers tracing the faded ink. A bubbling feeling rose in my throat; this gesture, this unexpected connection from someone from my past, made everything feel vibrant and full of possibility. I couldn't stop smiling, a genuine, unrestrained expression spreading across my features.

[story 3]

The doorbell rang, and I opened the door to find Leo standing there, holding a bouquet of sunflowers. My breath hitched. He looked so happy, and the sheer delight radiating off him was infectious. "For you, Mrs. Albright," he said, his voice warm and clear. As he presented the arrangement, I took the flowers, my grip tight. A warmth spread through my chest, chasing away any lingering fatigue. I couldn't help but laugh—a full, bright sound that surprised even myself. I felt an urge to immediately start planning how I would use this wonderful surprise.

"For you, Mrs. Albright," he said, his voice warm and clear. As he presented the arrangement, I took the flowers, my grip tight. A warmth spread through my chest, chasing away any lingering fatigue.
